data.title: Nigeria files $81.5 billion lawsuit against Binance
data.description:
Author: Vince Quill, CoinTelegraph; Translator: Baishui, Golden Finance

The Nigerian government has filed an $81.5 billion lawsuit against Binance, the world's largest centralized exchange, demanding it to pay $2 billion in back taxes and $79 billion in economic losses because Binance allegedly devalued Nigeria's local legal currency.

According to Reuters, Nigeria's Federal Inland Revenue Service (FIRS) said Binance has a "significant economic presence" in Nigeria and owes taxes for 2022 and 2023. FIRS also reportedly demanded that the company pay 26.75 percent interest on the taxes owed and went on to claim that the exchange played a major role in reducing the value of the Nigerian naira in the foreign exchange market.

The West African country previously charged and detained two Binance executives, Tigran Gambaryan and Nadeem Anjarwalla, on charges of tax fraud and money laundering. FIRS later dropped the tax charges against the two executives.

Since 2019, the Naira M2 money supply has increased significantly. Source: Trading Economics

Nigeria and Binance are embroiled in a high-profile legal dispute

In February 2024, the Nigerian government unexpectedly detained Gambaryan and Anjarwalla during routine talks between the company and national regulators.

Binance halted all naira trading activity on its platform in March 2024 after the two executives were detained and Nigerian officials complained that traders on the platform were destabilizing the naira.

Gambaryan’s detention attracted media attention amid reports that he suffers from a chronic illness but was reportedly denied adequate medical care.

In July 2024, U.S. Representative Rich McCormick introduced a resolution that classified the detention of American citizen Gambaryan as a hostage situation and called for the executive's immediate release.

Gambaryan sat in the courtroom, visibly shocked by the trial process and the poor prison conditions. Source: Associated Press

U.S. diplomats have stepped up pressure on the Nigerian government to release the Binance executive in September 2024, including a call from U.S. Ambassador to Nigeria Richard Mills to free Gambaryan .

Following a pressure campaign, the country’s Economic and Financial Crimes Commission dropped money laundering charges against Binance executives in October 2024.

Gambaryan was released from Nigeria on October 23, 2024, after spending nearly seven months in prison and a complex legal battle.

data.title: Musk's xAI launches "the most powerful AI on Earth"
data.description:
xAI, Elon Musk’s artificial intelligence company, unveiled its latest AI chatbot, Grok3.

Grok3 is a major leap forward in science, math, and creativity compared to its predecessor.

During the live demonstration, Musk highlighted the chatbot’s dramatic progress, saying, “We’re very excited to show you Grok3, which has leapfrogged its capabilities in a short period of time over the previous generation, Grok2.”

The event attracted nearly 100,000 viewers online, demonstrating the huge popularity of Grok3. Musk also thanked the development team for their hard work: "I feel very honored to work with such an excellent team."

01. The name "Grok" and its meaning

Musk also explained the origin of the name "Grok", saying that it was inspired by Robert Heinlein's science fiction novel "Stranger in a Strange Land".

In the novel, a character who grew up on Mars used the word "grok", which means to fully and deeply understand something.

“Empathy is an important part of understanding,” Musk explained. This is also the core quality that Grok3 pursues - establishing an emotional connection based on deep understanding.

02. Grok3 Development Process

Developing Grok3 was not easy. xAI executives talked about the huge challenges of building the infrastructure required to support such an advanced artificial intelligence system.

“We were on a tight schedule because we wanted to launch Grok3 as soon as possible,” said a team member.

They mentioned that they decided to build their own data center and completed the deployment of the first 100,000 GPUs in just 122 days, setting an industry record.

But the work did not stop there. In the following 92 days, the size of the H100 GPU cluster doubled, and the computing power was significantly improved, providing strong support for the continued optimization of Grok3.

“We’ve used this computing power to continuously improve our products along the way,” executives said.

03. Creative AI that “thinks”

One of the most striking features of Grok3 is its creativity.

During the demonstration, this AI showed amazing creative talent—it cleverly combined elements of Tetris and Puyo Puyo to design a new game.

This not only demonstrates Grok3’s strength in the field of technology, but also proves its unlimited potential in creative fields such as entertainment and art.

The xAI team said that Grok3 will be updated daily to ensure that it is always at the forefront of technology. This continuous optimization and upgrade mechanism allows Grok3 to continuously break through itself and maintain its leading edge.

In the future, Grok3's creative ability will not only be limited to game design, it may also bring revolutionary changes to film script creation, music production, art design and other fields.

With the continuous advancement of technology, this "thinking" AI may redefine human cognition of creativity.

04. Model and Performance

Grok3 is not a single model, but a family of models, each with its own unique features.

Grok3 mini is a streamlined version of the model that provides faster responses, but may compromise accuracy.

The other two versions, Grok3 Reasoning and Grok3 mini Reasoning, are designed to carefully "think" about the problem before giving a solution.

Users can use these reasoning models to solve complex problems through the Grok application.

xAI claims that Grok3 beat GPT-4o in benchmarks such as AIME and GPQA. Among them, AIME mainly tests the model's performance on mathematical problems, while GPQA focuses on doctoral-level scientific problems.

Early versions of Grok3 also achieved competitive results in Chatbot Arena, a crowdsourced testing platform that compares multiple AI models based on user-preferred answers.

In addition, xAI said that Grok3 Inference beat the highest version of o3-mini, o3-mini high, in multiple popular benchmarks.

05. Future Updates

Musk announced that Grok3 will launch a "voice mode" in the coming weeks. The model will also be integrated into xAI’s enterprise API via the DeepSearch feature.

Grok3 will initially be available to Premium Plus subscribers of the X platform.

A new subscription service called SuperGrok has also been launched for the Grok.com website and the Grok mobile app.

In a few months, xAI plans to open source Grok2, following their usual practice of open sourcing the previous version after the new version is fully launched.

Original source:

data.title: Binance sued in Nigeria; MiCA approves 10 stablecoin issuers
data.description:
Headlines

▌Binance is sued in Nigeria and asked to pay up to $81 billion in fines

Nigerian authorities sued cryptocurrency exchange Binance for more than $79 billion in economic losses and $2 billion in back taxes. The amount is almost 19 times the $4.3 billion fine Binance paid in 2023 after admitting to violating U.S. banking laws. The Federal Inland Revenue Service (FIRS), the Nigerian tax authority, also reportedly claimed that Binance was operating illegally in the country.

▌EU MiCA approves 10 stablecoin issuers, Tether is not on the list

The list of compliant stablecoin issuers under the EU Crypto Asset Market Regulation Act (MiCA) was announced, and 10 institutions including Circle, Crypto.com, and Societe Generale were approved to issue euro and dollar stablecoins, involving a total of 10 euro stablecoins and 5 dollar stablecoins. The world's largest stablecoin issuer Tether (USDT, with a market value of over $141 billion) did not appear on the list.

    Market

    As of press time, according to Coingecko data:

    BTC's latest transaction price is $96,616.72, with a daily increase or decrease of +1.1%;

    ETH's latest transaction price is $2,715.90, with a daily increase or decrease of +1.7%;

    BNB's latest transaction price is $651.11, with a daily increase or decrease of +0.6%；

    SOL's latest transaction price is 169.03 US dollars, with a daily increase or decrease of -0.3%；

    DOGE's latest transaction price is 0.2549 US dollars, with a daily increase or decrease of +1.3%；

    XPR's latest transaction price is 2.74 US dollars, with a daily increase or decrease of +6.8%。

    Policy

    ▌US President Trump: Will announce tariffs on cars, semiconductors, chips, pharmaceuticals, and possibly wood within a month

    US President Trump said that he would announce tariffs on cars, semiconductors, chips, pharmaceuticals, and possibly wood within the next month or earlier.

    ▌Trump: The Biden administration’s war on Bitcoin and cryptocurrency has ended

    US President Trump said that I have signed an executive order to put the United States at the forefront of artificial intelligence and end the Biden administration’s war on Bitcoin and cryptocurrency, marking the advent of a new era of digital assets.

    ▌President of The ETF Store: The US SEC has recognized the 19b-4 document submitted by Cboe

    Nate Geraci, president of The ETF Store, said on the social platform that the SEC has recognized the 19b-4 document submitted by Cboe, which seeks approval for the pledge function in the 21Shares Core Ethereum ETF.

    ▌Trump: Consider distributing 20% ​​of the funds saved by the Department of Government Efficiency to Americans

    US President Trump said that he is working hard to balance the budget immediately. I hope to achieve a balanced budget, but I don’t want to make a commitment now. Trump said that he is considering distributing 20% ​​of the funds saved by the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) to Americans, and he may also use 20% of the funds saved by DOGE to repay debts.

    ▌U.S. Department of Justice: Prosecutors file civil forfeiture lawsuit against a crypto investment fraud case to recover crypto assets

    According to the official website of the U.S. Department of Justice, U.S. prosecutors filed a civil forfeiture lawsuit against a crypto investment fraud case to recover 7.24 BTC, 14,120 USDT, 105.75 ETH and other crypto assets. The investigation showed that a Massachusetts resident was lured into a fake investment group and was deceived into transferring funds to a suspicious trading platform. Law enforcement agencies found that at least three victims in the United States remitted money to the same platform, and some of the funds were seized in October 2024. The prosecutor emphasized that the use of telecommunications means to defraud or conceal criminal proceeds are federal crimes, and the relevant assets will be returned to the victims after the civil lawsuit is concluded.

    ▌U.S. SEC Solicits Public Comments on WisdomTree XRP ETF Proposal

    The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) formally accepted the WisdomTree XRP ETF application on Wednesday and solicited public comments. The SEC requires relevant comments to be submitted within 21 days of publication in the Federal Register. The agency may decide to approve, reject or "initiate further review procedures."

    ▌Montana Strategic Bitcoin Reserve Legislation Has Passed Committee Review and Is About to Enter the House of Representatives Voting Stage

    According to market news, Montana's Strategic Bitcoin Reserve Legislation has passed committee review and is about to enter the House of Representatives voting stage.  

    As previously reported, on February 9, Montana House Bill No. 429 will authorize the state treasurer to invest up to $50 million from the general fund in "digital assets with a market value of more than $750 billion," namely Bitcoin, by July 15, 2025. These investments must be held by a qualified custodian or made through a trading platform trading fund.

      Blockchain Applications

      ▌FOX reporter: Canary Capital launches AXL Trust, the first trust to provide blockchain interoperability investment

      FOX Business reporter Eleanor Terrett shared in a post that Canary Capital launched the AXL (Axelar) Trust Fund, Canary AXL Trust, which will contain the native token of the Axelar network and is the first investment trust to provide general blockchain interoperability protocol investment.  

      - Axelar Protocol has emerged as a leading cross-chain protocol and is working closely with Ripple to support interoperability of its stablecoin RLUSD.  

      - Axelar acts as a bridge between the XRP Ledger and the XRP Ledger EVM, an Ethereum-compatible network.  

      - The Trust will provide institutional investors with exposure to blockchain interconnected technologies, connecting Web3 ecosystems such as XRP Ledger, Hedera, Stellar, TON, Sui, Solana and Bitcoin.

        Cryptocurrency

        ▌Coinbase CEO: I am not a Meme coin trader, and Coinbase adheres to the "free market" principle in listing coins

        Coinbase CEO Brian Armstrong expressed his views on the Meme coin craze on the X platform. He said that although he is not a Meme coin trader, he believes that Meme coins have become a phenomenon that cannot be ignored in the field of cryptocurrency. From the early Dogecoin to the various Meme coins today, although these tokens seem to be "toys" at the beginning, they may evolve into more powerful tools over time.  

        Regarding Coinbase's listing standards, Brian Armstrong said that from the first principle, we believe in free market capitalism. If our customers want it and it is legal, our goal is to let them make their own choices. But our job is to provide them with the best information we can find so that they can make an informed choice. If the token is a scam or fraud, we will delete it. But if it's just low quality, customers should be able to see the review or community notes and make their own choice. We want to provide consumers with useful information, not play judge and jury, or endorse/recommend investments.

        ▌Strategy has about 1,400 institutional shareholders holding about $34 billion worth of MSTR shares

        While Michael Saylor-led Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy) accelerated its purchases of Bitcoin in the fourth quarter of 2024, institutional investment managers were also aggressively increasing their holdings of MSTR shares.  

        Institutional investment managers who manage at least $100 million in equity assets continued to increase their holdings of MSTR in the fourth quarter, according to the latest 13F report filed. Fintel data shows that Strategy currently has about 1,400 institutional shareholders holding about $34 billion worth of MSTR shares. Vanguard, the world's second-largest asset manager, sold about 1.5 million shares of MSTR between the third and fourth quarters, but still ranked as the largest shareholder with a position of 16.3 million shares. Capital International Investors held 12.5 million shares, followed by BlackRock with 11.2 million shares.  

        It is worth noting that institutions also hold nearly $37 billion worth of MSTR options, doubling from the previous quarter. Susquehanna International Group holds 16.8 million put options, followed by Citadel Advisors with 13.6 million put options. Meanwhile, Jane Street holds 13.6 million call options, showing a more optimistic market expectation.

        ▌Billionaire Mark Cuban suspends Meme coin plan, saying major changes are needed

        Billionaire Mark Cuban announced the suspension of Meme coin plan, saying major changes are needed.  

        On January 21, Mark Cuban wrote: "If Meme coins are feasible, maybe I will issue one. Same terms as TRUMP, 20% circulating supply, same release schedule. There is one difference, that is, all revenue from the sale of tokens goes to the U.S. Treasury. The wallet address will be published so that everyone can track it. If you want to gamble, come on. But at least use it to reduce U.S. debt."

        ▌Institutional Bitcoin spot ETF holdings in Q4 2024 increased by more than 200% compared with Q3

        According to the 13F filing data submitted by the institution to the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), large institutions such as pension funds or hedge funds tripled the size of spot Bitcoin ETFs held in the fourth quarter of 2024.  

        As of Q4 2024, institutional investors held $38.7 billion worth of spot Bitcoin ETFs. That’s more than three times the amount reported in Q3, which reported just $12.4 billion in holdings.  

        Among them, the Wisconsin Investment Board increased its holdings of BlackRock iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) to just over 6 million shares. Billionaire hedge fund investor Paul Tudor also nearly doubled his stake in IBIT from 4,428,230 shares to 8,048,552 shares.  

        IBIT now has 1,100 institutional holders who have reported their holdings through 13F filings, according to Eric Balchunas, senior ETF analyst at Bloomberg Intelligence. Most newly launched ETFs typically have fewer than 10 institutional holders.

        ▌Vitalik Buterin: Ethereum is not a commercial company. If Ethereum becomes a company, it will lose its meaning of existence

        In a text AMA held on the Tako platform, Ethereum co-founder Vitalik Buterin responded to the question "Do you think Ethereum needs more commercial management?" He said: "I think Ethereum is a decentralized ecosystem, not a company. If Ethereum becomes a company, we will lose most of the meaning of Ethereum's existence. In fact, there are many large companies in the Ethereum ecosystem: Consensys, various client teams (Nethermind, Nimbus, etc.), Coinbase, L2 teams (including Aztec and intmax, their privacy technology is very interesting and underestimated by many people). The best way is to find ways to give these companies more opportunities to realize the advantages of the company, and the foundation plays a coordinating role. ”

        Important Economic Dynamics

        ▌Fed Bostic: Confidence in the economic outlook for 2025 has decreased

        Fed Bostic said that confidence in the economic outlook for 2025 has decreased. It is difficult to take all potential policy changes into account in the economic outlook; inflation progress is not expected to be a straight line. The Fed did not cut interest rates significantly last year, and the policy remains restrictive; it is satisfied with the suspension of interest rate cuts to observe economic development. The Fed is approaching the threshold level of its balance sheet, and the debt ceiling is a factor in determining this; it hopes to pay attention to the balance sheet on a daily basis to ensure that asset shrinkage is not excessive.

        ▌The probability of the Fed keeping interest rates unchanged in March is 97.5%

        According to CME's "Fed Watch": The probability of the Fed keeping interest rates unchanged in March is 97.5%, and the probability of a 25 basis point rate cut is 2.5%. The probability of maintaining the current interest rate unchanged by May is 88.9%, the probability of a cumulative 25 basis point rate cut is 10.8%, and the probability of a cumulative 50 basis point rate cut is 0.2%.

        ▌Fed Vice Chairman Jefferson: Monetary policy remains restrictive

        Fed Vice Chairman Jefferson said that monetary policy remains restrictive. The US economy is performing quite strongly; the US labor market is solid, inflation has slowed but is still high, and the road back to the 2% inflation target may be bumpy; the Fed can wait when considering the next monetary policy; the Fed's interest rate cut is reducing actual borrowing costs.

        ▌The three major U.S. stock indexes all closed slightly higher

        The three major U.S. stock indexes all closed slightly higher, with the S&P 500 up 0.24%, continuing to hit a record high; the Dow Jones Industrial Average up 0.16%, and the Nasdaq up 0.07%. Intel fell more than 6%. Palantir fell about 10%, the biggest single-day drop since August last year.

        ▌Analysts: Under many influencing factors, U.S. stocks still tend to be positive

        On Wednesday, U.S. stocks fluctuated between slight gains and slight losses as investors analyzed the minutes of the Federal Reserve's January policy meeting and digested the tariff measures announced by Trump. The S&P 500 is expected to set a record closing high for the second consecutive day, the Nasdaq was basically flat, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average fell slightly. "The market is still leaning toward the positive side in the face of high valuations, lower first-quarter earnings expectations and uncertainty about global trade," said Sam Stovall, chief investment strategist at CFRA Research in New York. Policymakers expressed concerns about stubborn inflation and the impact Trump's policy proposals, especially tariffs, could have on their efforts to reduce price growth to their targets after minutes from the Fed's January meeting showed. "The Fed will continue to be data-dependent and they are in no rush to cut rates. (There's really) nothing new, but at the same time there's nothing unexpectedly negative," Stovall added.

        ▌Fed Minutes: Want to see further progress on inflation before cutting rates

        The Fed wants to see "further progress on inflation" before deciding to cut rates again, according to the minutes. The Fed held interest rates steady in a range of 4.25%-4.5% at its last meeting, citing rising inflationary pressures, and markets expect no rate cut at its March meeting either. According to the minutes, the committee unanimously agreed that "the Federal Reserve has ample time to assess the changing outlook for economic activity, the labor market, and inflation." Many economists and market strategists worry that tariffs and stricter immigration policies could increase inflationary pressures, potentially offsetting the positive effects of lower taxes and looser regulations. The Fed has previously indicated that it is in no hurry to cut interest rates further due to persistently high inflation. Inflation has retreated from its post-pandemic highs but remains stubbornly above the Fed's ideal. For example, the consumer price index rose 3% year-on-year in January, the fastest pace in seven months and above the Fed's 2% target.

                  Golden Encyclopedia

                  ▌What is Fractal Bitcoin

                  Fractal Bitcoin is an innovative blockchain expansion solution based on recursive virtualization technology. It enhances the scalability of Bitcoin by creating a multi-layer network structure while maintaining a secure connection with the main network. The creation background of Fractal Bitcoin stems from the emergence of the Ordinals protocol and related applications in 2023. For security reasons, Bitcoin has imposed restrictions on opcodes and block storage space in its iterations. When the Bitcoin chain ecosystem develops rapidly, there are limitations in chain computing power and block space, so developers have proposed the concept of fractal Bitcoin, hoping to solve the shortcomings of the Bitcoin network.

                  Disclaimer: As a blockchain information platform, Golden Finance publishes articles for information reference only and is not intended as actual investment advice. Please establish a correct investment concept and be sure to increase risk awareness.

data.title: Interpreting the potential impact of the pledge mechanism on the development of ETH ETF from three perspectives
data.description:
Author: tomwanhh Translator: Vernacular Blockchain

ETH pledge ETF is about to be launched: it is expected to add approximately 2 million ETH to the pledge.

  • This article is an analysis of its potential impact:

  • ETH ETF inflow and issuer

  • Ethereum staking ecosystem

  • The impact of future PoS ETFs

1. Current status of ETH ETF: weak capital inflow

According to the market value ratio, ETH ETF should account for 16% of the $114 billion asset management scale of BTC ETF, but currently it is only 52% of the expected scale.

What is missing? Staking.

Narratives and price action will undoubtedly affect inflows, but the fundamental problem is the lack of collateral.

In the crypto community, the distinction between Bitcoin and Ethereum is very clear: one is digital gold, the other is the global computer. However, in the suit-and-tie financial circles, cryptocurrencies usually only account for 5% of their overall portfolio.

They are still learning about Bitcoin and need more time to understand Ethereum. A simple way to distinguish is the yield.

Institutional-grade Ethereum staking is already fairly mature in Europe. With the launch of the ETH ETF in the U.S., investors have less incentive to switch to non-staking ETFs.

Even if staking rewards are not enough to fully cover fees, they can almost offset them.

However, this is about to change. @21Shares has submitted a request to enable staking. That’s not all:

The SEC’s Crypto Task Force has invited @buffalu__, @RebeccaRettig1, and @KyleSamani to discuss staking, and even LST (Liquid Staking Tokens).

The Pectra upgrade is coming in April and is expected to shorten the unbonding period for Ethereum stakes, which was a significant hurdle when staking was initially introduced.

After adding staking, I expect the ETH ETF's asset management scale (AUM) to have the potential to catch up with the market capitalization ratio of Bitcoin and Ethereum, reaching $19 billion (an increase of $9 billion).

The competition for crypto ETFs in the United States is extremely fierce, with management fees close to 0%. With the introduction of staking income, investors not only do not have to pay fees, but may also receive 1%-3% income from staking.

Currently, there are a total of 3.9 million ETH in all ETFs. Assuming a 50% collateralization rate, this means that approximately 2 million ETH will be staked.

It is unrealistic to expect 100% of the ETH from ETFs to be staked. Because ETFs need to complete creation and redemption within a maximum of 7 days. Without a liquid staking solution, it is difficult for ETFs to achieve near 100% utilization.

Therefore, I expect ETFs to start with lower experimental utilization rates and gradually increase to 50%-75% utilization rates as the market matures.

2. The impact of future PoS ETFs

The introduction of staking may change the market landscape. In Bitcoin ETFs, competition mainly revolves around management fees. For ETH staking ETFs, competition will not only focus on management fees, but also on staking returns and liquidity management.

There is no doubt that giants like Blackrock and Fidelity can get competitive pricing from staking providers. Smaller issuers will need to find ways to either be cost efficient or perform better.

Bitwise made a smart acquisition and leveraging an in-house solution is probably the most cost effective way. Not sure if Attestant will be a whitelisted/compliant provider though.

For other issuers, I think they will turn to common institutional staking providers like @coinbase, @Figment_io, @BlockdaemonHQ, @peertopeervalidator, and @Kiln_finance.

Coinbase and other staking pools will become stronger. While the market share of liquid staking may decline in the short term, its growth is inevitable in the long run.

Pectra will be a catalyst to promote the development of staking. I expect ETH staking ETFs to gradually emerge within 3 to 12 months after the upgrade. How Pectra can benefit ETH staking ETFs:

  1. Higher effective balances (32 -> 2048): This will reduce the cost of staking by reducing the number of validators required.

  2. Lower initial slashing penalties: This will reduce stakers’ risk.

  3. Shorter unbonding period: Validators can be merged, thus reducing the number of validators waiting in line for partial redemption.

3. ETH ETF Inflows and Issuers

Liquid Staking (LST) is clearly superior to traditional staking solutions. LST can not only enter the primary market, but also the secondary market. If a large number of redemptions occur, the issuer can:

  • Sell it on an exchange to get instant liquidity

  • Use it as collateral to borrow ETH

  • In major markets, established protocols like Lido, with their liquidity buffers, can even offer faster activation queues and shorter redemption times.

While I think Lido’s stETH may become the preferred solution for an ETF due to its market dominance, liquidity, and institutional products, the following Liquid Staking Tokens (LST) should also be considered:

  • lsETH from @liquid_col, which is integrated with Figment

  • cbETH from Coinbase

  • rETH from @Rocket_Pool, which supports decentralization

If the ETH ETF enables staking, then the SOL ETF can also have staking features at launch.

Without staking, investors will miss out on 7%-10% returns. Additionally, SOL’s unbonding period (approximately 2.5 days) is much shorter than ETH, making it easier to enable staking.

data.title: I joined the National Army in 1949. How is Virtuals doing now?
data.description:
Author: Luke, Mars Finance

While Deepseek and Grok3 continue to set off a craze in the Web2 world, the AI ​​ecosystem of Web3 is experiencing its largest retracement in history. Many AI tokens have retreated at least 80% from their highs a month ago. The Virtual Protocol (VIRTUAL) ecosystem has also seen a sharp drop - the VIRTUAL platform coin plummeted 8.6% in a single day, and its incubated tokens such as GAME and LUNA fell by more than 10%.

Previously, Virtuals' deployment on Solana was seen as a positive by the market, but now it reminds people of the new recruits who had just joined the army in 1949, with great ambitions, but found themselves pushed onto the retreating train at the first moment of the battlefield.

But all this was not without signs. From the peak in January to the trough in February, Virtuals' experience is just like a crypto version of "The Big Short", revealing the cruel game between speculative bubbles, technological ideals and market cycles. As the largest AI agent platform on Ethereum's Base ecosystem, Virtuals had planned to expand its business to Solana, trying to replicate the success of AI agents in Solana's ecosystem. However, Solana's recent scandals, especially the turmoil surrounding Meme coins and conspiracy groups, have plunged Solana's ecosystem into a crisis of trust. Although Virtuals seems to be looking for new opportunities, the plunge in the entire AI agent track and the entire altcoin market shows that the market winter seems to be accelerating for both Solana and Ethereum ecosystems.

1. Solana branch opening was cold: multi-chain expansion "not suitable"

On January 25, Virtuals announced its entry into the Solana ecosystem, and the market once regarded this move as an important step in the "AI agent empire". The team plans to replicate the successful model on the Base chain through five major plans, including the Meteora trading pool and the SOL Strategic Reserve (SSR). However, the data from Solana’s debut on February 12 burst all these bubbles:

The AI ​​agent graduation rate is only 8.3%: In the Solana ecosystem, only 13 of the 156 new projects have reached the graduation standard, of which more than half of the projects are close to zero, and only 5 have a market value of over one million US dollars;

Liquidity dilemma: User participation on the Solana chain is sluggish, and the number of holding addresses of most projects is less than 100, which is in sharp contrast to the 222,000 active wallets on the Base chain.

This situation is like a popular restaurant blindly expanding its branches - it originally thought that it could conquer new markets with its signature dishes (AI agents), but it was found that Solana users prefer local characteristics (such as Meme coin $TRUMP and local projects such as ai16z). What’s even more ironic is that Virtuals’ “inter-chain abstract exchange” solution designed for cross-chain is still at the white paper stage. Users can only use SOL to purchase AI agents on Base, or use ETH to purchase Solana agents. The operation threshold is comparable to asking Sichuan chefs to make sushi.

2. AI agent retreat: from “technological revolution” to “leek toys”

Virtuals’ dilemma is essentially a microcosm of the bursting of the bubble in the AI ​​agent track. According to Cookie.fun, the market value of the entire AI agent track has shrunk by 65.3% in the past month. This trend is consistent with Messari's earlier prediction: "The prosperity of AI agent frameworks will peak in Q1 2025 due to the lack of real demand support." During the boom period, the madness of the AI ​​agent track was like the "Trump pump" in the stock market. In December 2024, thanks to the AI ​​narrative and the "Trump pump", the VIRTUAL token soared 500% in 30 days, and its market value once exceeded US$5 billion, becoming the "Nasdaq of AI agents." At the same time, the platform's cumulative revenue exceeded $37.76 million, and its trading volume reached $6.74 billion, as if it had become a star in the crypto industry overnight.

However, as the bubble burst, the cost of swimming naked began to emerge:

  • Token economy involution: The $48 million buyback plan launched in January did raise the price of the currency in the short term, but it also exposed the vulnerability of relying on the commission sharing - when the trading volume plummeted, the destruction mechanism became a "blood pump" instead;

  • Agent homogeneity: Most of the 16,000 AI agents on the platform are social KOL tokens (such as AIXBT) or "virtual idols" who are riding on the hot spots. Their functions are limited to forwarding tweets or generating earthy love words, and they are still a long way from "disruptive AI". At the same time, the number of new agents is also shrinking rapidly, with only 4 new agents online on February 18.

This situation reminds people of the craze for NFT avatars in 2021 - when speculators find that the "digital monkeys" in their hands can neither chat nor move bricks, a sell-off becomes inevitable.

3. Ethereum to the left, Solana to the right: Prisoner’s dilemma under ecological internal friction

Virtuals’ cross-chain strategy was originally intended to simultaneously deploy Base (Ethereum L2) and Solana, two major public chains, but this plan exposed its dilemma of being caught in a two-sided trap in the competition among public chains, like a sandwich.

  • The embarrassment of the Base chain: As an “excellent student” in the Ethereum ecosystem, Virtuals’ expansion to Solana was instead seen by the Ethereum community as a “betrayal” of the parent chain. This multi-chain layout has exacerbated the Ethereum community’s anxiety about the “blood-sucking effect” of L2, especially at a time when Ethereum itself is struggling to deal with scalability and cost issues.

  • Solana’s cold reception: Although Virtuals incentivizes the Solana ecosystem by converting 1% of the handling fee into SOL, Solana users clearly prefer local projects, especially homegrown competitors such as ai16z (market value of $2.55 billion). As an "outsider", Virtuals has always found it difficult to gain widespread recognition in the Solana community and has almost become an "airborne soldier."

This situation reminds people of a son-in-law who tries to please both his mother-in-law and his mother-in-law at the same time. In the end, no matter which side is dissatisfied, there is only one possible result: being kicked out.

Fourth, the Revelation of Retail Investors: The "Cognitive Folding" of Speculation and Value

For ordinary investors, the roller coaster market of Virtuals provides the market with several profound warnings:

  • Narrative ≠ Value: The grand vision of AI agency must be supported by actual landing scenarios. If the token economy relies solely on the circulation of transaction fees, it will eventually become a "casino on the chain" and cannot develop sustainably.

  • Multi-chain ≠ Security: Although cross-chain expansion seems to be able to open up the market, it will actually disperse the team's resources, especially in the downturn of the market. Excessive layout will only increase the risk of capital chain rupture-the more branches, the greater the crisis.

  • Whales ≠ Allies: Although VIRTUAL has been favored by large whale investments, these whales often sell more quickly than retail investors. In the past month, VIRTUAL has fallen by as much as 46.3%, and it is the rapid shipments of these whales that have exacerbated the panic in the market.

From this, we can see that there is often a huge cognitive gap between market speculation and the value of virtual assets. In the world of virtual assets, many investors have fallen into a "value illusion", thinking that the bubble will continue to expand, but eventually ushered in a ruthless "fission".

data.title: BTC on-chain data analysis: Has this cycle reached its peak?
data.description:
Guest: Colin Free Trader On-chain Data Researcher

Host: Alex Research Partner, Mint Ventures

Organized by: Mint Ventures

Hello everyone, welcome to WEB3 Mint To Be, initiated by Mint Ventures. Here, we continue to ask questions and think deeply, clarify facts, explore reality, and find consensus in the WEB3 world. Clarify the logic behind hot topics for everyone, provide insights that penetrate the events themselves, and introduce multiple perspectives.

Alex: This episode is a bit special, because we have discussed many topics about specific tracks or projects before, and also exchanged some cyclical narratives, such as memes we talked about before. But today we are going to discuss on-chain data analysis, especially on-chain data analysis of BTC. We will take a close look at its working principles, key indicators, and learn its methodology. In today's show, we will mention many concepts about indicators. These concepts are listed at the beginning of the text version for your convenience.

Some data indicators and concepts mentioned in this podcast:

Glassnode: A commonly used on-chain data analysis platform that requires payment.

Realized Market Price (Realized Price): Calculated based on the price weighted calculation of Bitcoin's last on-chain movement, reflecting the on-chain historical cost of Bitcoin, suitable for evaluating the overall profit/loss status of the market.

URPD: Realized Price Distribution. Used to observe the price distribution of BTC chips.

RUP (Relative unrealized profit): Relative unrealized profit. Used to measure the ratio of unrealized profits of all holders in the Bitcoin market to the total market value.

Cointime True Market Mean Price: An on-chain average price indicator based on the Cointime Economics system, which aims to more accurately evaluate the long-term value of BTC by introducing the "time weight" of Bitcoin. Compared with the current market price of BTC and the realized market price (Realized Price), the True Market Mean Price under the Cointime system also comprehensively considers the impact of time and is suitable for the price of BTC in the big cycle.

Shiller ECY: A valuation indicator proposed by Nobel Prize winner in Economics Robert Shiller to evaluate the long-term return potential of the stock market and measure the attractiveness of stocks relative to other assets. It is improved from the Shiller Price-to-Earnings Ratio (CAPE) and mainly considers the impact of the interest rate environment.

The opportunity to learn on-chain data analysis

Alex: Today we invited Colin, a free trader and on-chain data researcher. Let Colin say hello to our audience.

Colin:Hello everyone, first of all, thank you Alex for the invitation. I was a little surprised when I received this invitation, because I am an unknown small retail investor, and I don’t have any special titles. I just quietly do my own trading. My name is Colin, and I run an account on Twitter called Mr. Berg. I usually share some on-chain data teaching, analysis of the current market conditions, and some trading concepts. I have three definitions of myself: the first is an event-driven trader, and I usually think about event-driven trading strategies; the second is an analyst of on-chain data, which is also the content I usually share on Twitter; the third is more conservative, and I call myself an index investor. I will choose to allocate part of my funds to the US stock market, and use the Beta of this part of the funds to reduce the overall volatility of my asset curve, while maintaining a certain degree of defensiveness in the overall position. The above is roughly how I define myself.

Alex:Thanks for Colin's self-introduction. I invited Colin to participate in the program because I saw his on-chain data analysis of Bitcoin on Twitter, which was very inspiring. This is a topic that we rarely talked about before, and it is also a relatively lacking part in my own section. I read the series of articles he wrote and felt that the logic was clear and meaningful, so I invited him. I would like to remind everyone that today, both my and the guests' views are highly subjective in the program, and the information and views may change in the future. Different people may have different interpretations of the same data and indicators. This episode does not constitute any investment advice. This program will mention some data analysis platforms, which are only for personal sharing and examples, not for commercial recommendations. This program has not received any commercial sponsorship from any platform. Let's get to the point and talk about on-chain data analysis of crypto assets. Just now I mentioned that Colin is a trader, so under what circumstances did you start to get in touch with and learn on-chain data analysis of crypto assets?

Colin: I think this question should be divided into two parts to answer. First of all, I think that no matter who is around, as long as they want to enter or have entered the financial market, including myself, the most important goal should be to make money and use profits to improve their quality of life. So my philosophy has always been very consistent, that is, I will learn whatever can help me make money. In this way, I will improve the expected value of my overall trading system. In simple terms, I will learn whatever can make money. The second part is that I was exposed to on-chain data by accident at the beginning. About six or seven years ago, I didn't understand it at all, and I looked at this and that. When exploring various fields, I saw very interesting research theories and wanted to learn them. At that time, I accidentally saw that Bitcoin had a so-called on-chain data analysis field, so I started to study and research it. In the later stage of learning, I will combine the knowledge learned in other fields, mainly the part of quantitative trading development, and combine it with on-chain data, and then develop some trading models, and finally integrate these models into my own trading system.

Alex: So how many years have you been systematically studying and researching on-chain data analysis since you officially started to get involved?

Colin: I think this is difficult to define. In fact, I have never really learned it systematically. Because from the past to now, I have encountered a problem myself, that is, I have not seen any systematic teaching at all. From the very beginning when I saw this field, it may really be several years ago. I discovered it at that time, but I didn’t study it in depth. I just read two or three articles to know about this thing. After a while, I came back and saw some more in-depth content. At that time, I was focusing on studying other things. I came back here and saw that this was quite interesting, so I continued to study. There was no time for systematic learning, just patching things together.

Alex: I understand. So how long has it been since you learned about on-chain data and applied it to your actual investment practice?

Colin: This boundary is difficult to define, but I think it is close to two rounds of Bitcoin cycles... It can't be counted as two rounds, it depends on whether you define it from the bull market or the bear market. I started to get in touch with it around 2020 or 2019, but there was no actual application at that time because I didn't dare. At that time, I was not very familiar with this thing, but I had already started to learn.

The value and principle of on-chain data analysis

Alex: I understand. We will talk about many specific concepts about on-chain data analysis, including some indexes. What are the on-chain data observation platforms you use daily?

Colin:I mainly use a website now, which is Glassnode. Let me briefly talk about it. It needs to be paid. There are two paid levels. One is the professional version, which is more expensive. I remember it cost more than 800 US dollars a month. The second one I forgot a little bit, about 30 to 40 U a month. It also has a free version, but the free version actually has very little information. Of course, there are many other ones besides Glassnode. I finally chose it because this website was the most suitable for my appetite when I was screening and researching at the beginning.

Alex:I see. After reading a lot of information from Colin, I also registered for Glassnode and became their paid member. I really feel that their data is very rich, and the timeliness is also better. Now let's talk about the second question. You just mentioned that you are a trader, and you value its help in investment practice. So what is the core value of on-chain data analysis in your investment? What is the principle behind it? Please introduce it to us.

Colin:Okay. First of all, let's talk about the first one, which is the value and principle of on-chain data analysis. I plan to talk about these two together because they are actually quite simple. Our traditional financial market, whether it is trading stocks, futures, bond options, or even real estate, or some raw materials, has a fundamental difference from Bitcoin, which is that it uses blockchain technology. The most important and most often mentioned value of this technology is its transparency. All of this Bitcoin transfer information is open and transparent, so you can directly see on the chain, for example, 300 Bitcoins are transferred from one address to another, which can be checked on the blockchain browser. Although I have no way of knowing who is behind this string of addresses, it is not important because in fact no single individual can affect the price trend and trend of the entire Bitcoin. So normally, when we study on-chain data, we look at the overall market, its trends, and the consensus and behavior of the group. Even if I don't know who is behind this address or that address, I can analyze the flow of their chips by summarizing all the addresses to see whether they have taken profits or stopped losses, their profit situation, their loss situation, at which price they are more inclined to buy a large amount of Bitcoin or at which price they don't like to buy Bitcoin. These data are actually visible. This is what I think is the greatest value of Bitcoin on-chain data analysis compared to other financial markets, because other markets can't do this.

Alex: Indeed, this is very important. When we do crypto investment, we also need to analyze the fundamentals like we look at stocks or other products. As you just said, on-chain data is transparent and everyone can observe it. If other professional investors look at on-chain data and you don't, it is equivalent to you losing an important weapon in investment.

Difficulties of on-chain data analysis

Alex: When you actually do on-chain data analysis, what do you think are the main difficulties and challenges?

Colin:I think this question is very well asked, and I plan to answer it in two parts. First of all, the first part is relatively easy to solve. There is a more difficult point in learning, which is the basic knowledge. For most people, including me at that time, because I mentioned it before, it is difficult to find a truly systematic teaching. Of course, I didn’t ask offline if there are any paid courses of this kind, but if there are, I probably wouldn’t dare to buy them, because I have been trading myself until now, and I don’t actually pay to buy some courses. I haven’t come into contact with any systematic teaching courses, so in fact, all the content must be explored and explored by myself. There are many types of on-chain data. In the process of research, my own philosophy is to understand the calculation methods and principles behind every indicator I have seen. This is actually a very time-consuming process, because if you only see a certain indicator, it will give you a calculation formula. My idea is to figure out what is behind this calculation formula and why it is designed in this way. After I figure out these indicators, the second thing I have to do is screening. If you have experience in quantitative strategy development or have studied indicators, you will know one thing, that is, the correlation of many indicators is very high. Too high correlation will cause a problem, that is, it is easy to generate noise in your judgment, or you will over-interpret. For example, suppose I have a system for escaping the top today. This system may have 10 signals from 1 to 10. If the correlation between 1 to 4 is too high, it will cause a problem. For example, if the price of Bitcoin has a certain behavior or change today, it may directly make 1 to 4 lights up at the same time, which is actually very troublesome. Because if they are too correlated, this is an inevitable phenomenon. If 4 out of 10 lights are on today, you say it is very dangerous, but in fact this is not very reasonable, because they will be on. If you don't cut them according to the correlation, this phenomenon is very easy to happen. After I studied the principles of each indicator and data, I can know whether they are highly correlated by looking at the calculation formula directly, and I cut them according to the correlation. For example, if these 5 correlations are very high, I will cut and filter them slightly, and finally select one or two.

The first part is actually easy to solve, and it is not the main difficulty. The second part is the real challenge, which is about the on-chain data. How do you prove your point of view to the people around you or to yourself? I may have to give a more vulgar example here, but it is easy to understand. I have written on Twitter before that in fact, the quantitative field will tell you that trading is not easy to stick to the old ways. Then I have given an example before, assuming that there is a very strange trading strategy today, and its entry standard is that if my dog ​​at home barks twice and it is raining outside, then I will enter the market and go long. As a result, I backtested this strategy 1,000 times and found that the winning rate was 95%, far outperforming the market. Does anyone dare to use this strategy? It's actually quite strange. When a dog barks for no reason and it's raining outside, you can go long, and the winning rate is still so high. There is actually a term for this called survivor bias. If you can't give it any logical support today, even if the sample size is sufficient, this strategy can't be used. Some people will argue that it has been backtested 1,000 times and has a winning rate of 95%. The backtest results support that this strategy can be used. I just mentioned the so-called survivor bias. Simply put, if I toss a coin 10 times in a row, the probability of getting heads 10 times is actually 1/1024. In other words, on average, when every 1,024 people do this, one person will succeed. The situation of throwing four heads in a row is actually the so-called survivor. The other 1,023 people failed when doing this. We don't actually see it. We always see those successful cases. Back to Alex's question, that is, where is the so-called main difficulty. Because we are mainly looking at large-scale consensus and trends, looking back at the history of Bitcoin, the three most obvious cycle tops are the two tops in 2013, 2017 and 2021. This is only 4 samples, which is absolutely not enough. Since the number of samples is not enough, if we go back to the old ways today and see where a certain indicator has been in 2013, where a certain indicator has been in 2017, so it is unreasonable to go there this year. Because the number of samples is completely insufficient, if we do not give it logic to do research at this time, your theory is very easy to make mistakes. One of the main problems is that in the face of such a small number of historical samples, I must use the deductive method instead of simply the inductive method to study. After I finish my research, I draw a conclusion based on the deductive method, and I need time to prove whether my view is right or wrong. If it is right, it means that my previous deductive reasoning process may be reasonable. If it is wrong, then I still need to continue to correct the previous deductive logic. But if we just rely on induction today, most retail investors like to do this the most. They think that the previous trend is very similar to the current trend, so there should be a surge or a plunge in the future. This is actually unreasonable. Back to the first sentence I said at the beginning, I think the biggest difficulty is that I have to prove to others or myself that my inference is correct, so I have to correct my logic and assumptions all the time, and then check whether there are any flaws. Because Bitcoin is too young, there will always be a problem of insufficient sample size in the on-chain data analysis. At this time, you actually have to use a simple deductive method in research, which is to use a logical way to infer it, and then wait for time to prove your judgment. This is the biggest difficulty I have encountered so far.

Focus on on-chain indicators

Alex:I understand. I think it is still very inspiring. The question I asked you just now was also some confusion when I started to look at various indicators on Glassnode. It has so many indicators, which indicator should I use as my trading reference? Because many indicators have various calculation logics. I tend to choose the logic of those indicators in the future, which is quite similar to the logic you just mentioned. First of all, I have to look at the calculation logic behind this indicator, and I have to think that this logic makes sense, rather than backtesting and extracting it as if this indicator is very accurate, and then use this accurate indicator to predict the future. As you said, the reference of the deductive method needs to be greater before it can be used as the main indicator we adopt. So after what you just talked about, in your current daily analysis of Bitcoin, which on-chain indicators have you been paying attention to for a long time or do you think are more important?

Colin: Actually, I have said this question before. I will try to select according to the correlation. I usually look at a lot of on-chain data indicators. Today, I will introduce them from different dimensions, that is, try to split them into three levels from the part with low correlation.

The first indicator that I will pay attention to for a long time and focus on must be the URPD indicator. It is a chart, which is presented in the form of a row of bars, with the horizontal axis being the price of Bitcoin and the vertical axis being the number of Bitcoins. Suppose we see a very high and large bar at 90,000 today, then we will know that a very large number of Bitcoins are built at this position, that is, the cost of their purchase, and the bar chart will show how many Bitcoins they bought at this price. So in fact, based on this incident, we can see at a glance that if there is a lot of accumulation above 100,000, then we can know that many people buy above 100,000. There are two main points of observation in this URPD chart. The first is the simplest chip structure. Suppose today I see that the current market situation is around 87,000, and a very large number of chips have been accumulated above 87,000. According to last week's data, it should be 4.4 million. Then we know that there is a very large turnover in this range, or someone has bought here. Since someone has bought, it is very likely to form a certain consensus. In this large accumulation range, it is easy to form an attractive effect on the price, that is, the price is likely to fluctuate in this range, and it is also easy to fall and then repair after a period of time, and then rise back. If the price goes up, the chips below have all become floating profits, then they are easy to sell, do short-term trading, and then sell back at a higher price. So it is easy to fluctuate in this range. This is the first observation point. The second observation point is that we can observe the distribution process of Bitcoin through URPD. The so-called distribution is that in the early bear market, those chips that bought Bitcoin at a low price, and then they sold the cheap chips in their hands upwards, so I define this process as distribution. Suppose today there are 300,000 more chips at the price of 100,000, and the chips with a cost of 20,000, assuming it is 20,000, just reduced by 300,000, then we can actually see that people with a cost of 20,000 sold 300,000 today, and their average selling price is about 100,000. We can see whether the low-cost chips usually have some drastic changes. Of course, the current price is 100,000 or 90,000, so if they change dramatically, it must be a decrease, not an increase, because the current price range is more than 90,000, not more than 20,000, so it will only decrease, not increase. So we can observe the distribution rate based on this matter, which is probably what I mean. This is the first indicator that I will pay attention to for a long time.

The second indicator I want to introduce is called RUP, which is called Relative Unprofitable Status in Chinese. This indicator actually has only one purpose, which is to help us measure the profitability of the overall market, that is, the profitability of the entire market corresponding to the current price of Bitcoin. For example, how much did you earn, or not much, or a lot, which is roughly this concept. The principle of this indicator is actually very simple, because through the so-called transparent mechanism of the blockchain, we can track the purchase price of most chips. We can compare the purchase price of these chips with the current price. Suppose he bought it at 50,000 and the current price is 100,000, we will know that this Bitcoin is currently profitable, so we can calculate how much money it earns. For example, if 10 bitcoins were bought at 50,000, and now they are 100,000, one bitcoin would earn 50,000, and 10 bitcoins would earn 500,000. We add up all these floating gains and losses, and then standardize this number according to the current market value, then we can get a number between 0 and 1. It is easy to observe between 0 and 1. Assuming that today's RUP is very high, such as 0.7, 0.68, or 0.75, then we know that the overall profitability of the market is very high, which may make more people want to take profits. Therefore, if the RUP is too high, it is usually regarded as a relative warning by us.

The third dimension I want to talk about is a fair valuation model of the market. There are actually many different bitcoin valuation models on the market, and each model actually uses a different method to evaluate the fair value of this bitcoin. The so-called fair value is actually how much a bitcoin is worth. After reading so many models, I think the Cointime Price model is the most proven. I haven't seen the Chinese translation of this term anywhere else. In short, we often hear a person named Cathie Wood. Her ARK Invest and the on-chain data website, which I just mentioned, Glassnode, mentioned this concept in a document produced by the two parties. The biggest feature of this model is that it introduces the concept of time weighting to calculate the fair value of Bitcoin. The calculated number has two main uses. The first is very simple, which is to buy at the bottom. Suppose today in the bear market, it falls and falls, and finally falls below the valuation given by Cointime Price. As I said just now, this number is actually how much a Bitcoin should be worth. If it falls below this position today, it is equivalent to buying at a very cost-effective position. According to historical backtesting and its logic, it can be seen that every time the price falls below the Cointime Price, it is actually a very good position to buy at the bottom. The second application is to escape the top. We can monitor the current price and the price of Cointime Price to see how far it is. If it deviates too much from the Coin Time Price, we can evaluate whether the market may be close to the top if the deviation is too large. The above three dimensions are the chip structure, profit status and fair valuation model, which are the three indicators and aspects I want to share.

How to view the data conflict

Alex:Okay, I have just made it very clear. Many users may ask a question. The three focus indicators you just listed may represent different aspects, which is consistent with what you just said that the correlation between them is not that high, so they can be put together as a reference indicator. Then suppose that such indicators have divergent situations in actual application. For example, indicator one may feel that it is currently in a distribution situation, while indicators two and three may show that the current distance to the top does not seem to be that high from a cyclical perspective. In this case, how would you deal with the data conflict?

Colin：I think this is not just in the field of on-chain data analysis, but also in other fields such as technical analysis or macroeconomics, where there may be so-called fighting. In the on-chain field, my personal approach is very simple. I will give different weights to different levels. What I value most is actually the chip structure, that is, the progress of distribution. Because in fact, in terms of profitability, it also helps me observe the low-cost chips in the market. During the bear market, for example, the Bitcoin chips bought at 15,000 or 16,000, have they been distributed? There is a very special phenomenon that in every cycle of Bitcoin over the years, there are actually two very obvious large-scale distributions. For example, in 2024, the most obvious case is from March to April last year. In fact, in terms of profitability, you can definitely see large-scale distributions at that time. But if I only see large-scale distributions today, then my next question is to think about whether they have been distributed? All the criteria for judgment start from this question. If they have a large-scale distribution, but have not yet completed the distribution, then I can tell myself with peace of mind that the bull market has not ended. For example, when I was in March and April last year, Bitcoin rushed to more than 70,000. I was actually quite excited because the bull market finally came and set a new high. As a result, it began to fluctuate for more than half a year. At that time, I could not draw the conclusion that the bottom had been reached by observing these data. At best, it was the first distribution. And a lot of data is also like this. For example, I have published some mid-term analysis and chip structure analysis before. At that time, according to the average cost of short-term holders, his situation was actually different from the end of the real bull market. So I was actually very at ease at that time. Then you say that the data is fighting, and he now says that the distribution has been completed, so should I flee the top? In fact, no, because the main question is still the one I just talked about: whether the distribution has ended. Using this question as the standard for screening each indicator and as the basis for judgment, it is actually very easy to draw this conclusion, that is, even if the distribution has occurred and is still large-scale, I just need to judge whether it has ended. Using this as a criterion can effectively deal with the so-called data conflict problem.

Alex:Let's now formulate a scenario. For example, we are currently looking at URPD. Assuming that this indicator has already had two distributions, it is more like what you just said, one in March and April last year, and then there was another peak of distribution from December to January at the end of the year. Assuming that it has such a distribution, but the other two valuation indicators may not be so high. When this happens, you just said that you will give them different weights.Will you reduce part of the position according to the proportion of the weight, or will you consider the three indicators in a unified way and not adjust the position according to the weight, but make one or two important decisions at the critical moment?

Colin:My own approach is the former, because in fact no one can know whether it is the real top now, and no one can escape to the highest position. If there is, it would be too powerful, and I would definitely want to know it. My personal interpretation of the top is that it is a slow process. Although it seems very fast when you look at the daily chart, if you are in the present moment, for example, if you are at 69,000, the top of the previous cycle, you will not feel that it is the top now. We can only make a judgment based on the data and say that the conditions for the top may be met now. So based on this premise, I will actually make a segmented position. For example, when I think the top conditions have gradually matured, once I see a certain indicator giving me a warning during this period, such as a RUP divergence I shared on Twitter before, I will make a corresponding reduction in positions. Of course, the extent of this reduction in positions must be determined in advance at the beginning. It is impossible to say that there is a divergence now and I don’t know how much to reduce. I will first make a rough plan. For example, I divide the position into 4 parts, and once a certain type of warning appears, I will reduce one of them first, and then reduce another one when the second warning comes out. At the same time, I will plan that the last part of funds must be sold no matter what. For example, if the bear market is definitely over, but other warning signs have not yet appeared, we need to develop an extreme, final escape strategy to screen.

Alex:I see. We will gradually exit and reduce our positions according to different warning signs.

Colin:Yes.

Judgment and basis of BTC's position in this cycle

Alex:I see. I have also been following your Twitter account recently. You also practice your trading practices based on the indicators just mentioned, including the concepts behind these indicators. Now let's look at Bitcoin. It has been fluctuating between 91,000 and 109,000 for almost three months. There are quite a lot of differences in the market about this price range. Unlike in December and January, everyone thinks that this bull market is far from over and will reach 150,000, 200,000 or even 300,000. There are many positive views. There are big differences in the market. Some people think that the top of BTC in this round is around 100,000, but some people think that BTC has not reached its peak in this cycle and there will still be a main uptrend in 2025. Based on your current comprehensive judgment, what is your opinion? Where is BTC in this round of big cycle? And what are the data sources that support your judgment?

Colin: Before answering this question, I may have to give a vaccination. I am actually very bearish on 2025. I think BTC is currently in a state where the conditions for the top are formed. In fact, I know that many people, including some participants around me, did not have good returns during the so-called special bull market in 2024, because the overall market performance in 2024 was different from every previous cycle. The most obvious point was that there was no altcoin season. This hurt many people, including some of my friends who are not professional traders. They also came to participate in this market. In fact, they suffered a lot of losses on altcoins. Why is this the case? Let's take a look back at 2024. There was an altcoin market at the beginning of the year, and the second time was in November last year, when Trump was elected as the US president. Compared with the previous cycles, these two altcoin markets actually have a big and obvious point, that is, their sustainability is not very good. Even in the market in November and December last year, altcoins did not rise across the board. It was a very obvious sector rotation. At that time, there was a Defi sector, and after the rise, it was replaced by old coins, such as XRP, and Litecoin, etc. The sector rotation was very obvious. From this incident, we can see that if everyone thinks that the bull market in 2024 is a bull market, this cycle is actually very different from the previous ones. There is also a theory that there will be a so-called cottage season before the end of the bull market. In fact, I personally think that you can't say that the bull market will end only when the cottage season appears. This is obviously not strongly correlated. We can't use this as a judgment on whether the bull market is over. As mentioned earlier, there is a shortcoming in chain data analysis, that is, the number of samples is never enough. If we simply use historical conditions to extrapolate today's market, it is actually a practice of carving a boat to find a sword, which is not very good. If you want to carve a boat to find a sword, the tops of 13, 17, and 21 should appear around the end of the year, according to the time.

I personally think that the conditions for the so-called top formation are now in place. The reasons are very complicated, and I use a lot of indicators and data to make judgments. Let me briefly talk about a few of the more core ones. The first one is the chip structure we just mentioned, that is, the URPD chart. We can see one thing, in 2022, and 2023, those low-cost chips accumulated, at that time they bought a lot of BTC at a low price, and so far, a lot of chips have been distributed. To put it bluntly, they have sold it, they are not playing anymore. Some listeners may have a question, that is, what does it have to do with me if they sell it? There is a concept that I may need to explain to you. At the end of each round of bull market, almost every time it is because those low-cost chips have been distributed, and then the bull market ends. There is a less intuitive point here. It is not because they smash the market that the bull market ends, but because the price has been rising, they sell all the way, and they sell all the way, and then the price stops, and the bull market will end. This is not just my head saying that it must be like this, there is a logic in it. Assume that every BTC chip involved in the market today is a high-cost chip, for example, those bought for more than 90,000, and those bought for 50,000, 20,000, and 30,000 have already run away. At this time, as long as the price does not show a very obvious or strong main rising wave, even if it is simply a so-called wide fluctuation, such as the fluctuation range between 70,000 and 50,000 last year, or the current fluctuation range between 90,000 and 109,000, these high-cost chips will be under great pressure to hold positions. The high pressure of holding positions will lead to a problem. The price is about 95,000 or 96,000 now. If it falls to 89,000 today, it is actually less than 10%, but the pressure on these chips is very high. Many of them are even short-term traders. Once the pressure is high, they may choose to sell. Then the selling will cause the price to fall further. If it falls, other high-cost chips will not be able to bear the pressure, and they will sell again, which will cause a chain reaction. This is what I think I can see from the URPD chart, that is, many low-cost chips have been distributed.

The second indicator I just mentioned is called RUP, which is an indicator used to measure the profitability of the market. If you are interested in this indicator, you can check it out. It is very interesting. If you put its line together with the price line, their correlation is very high, and they almost move together. This is actually a very reasonable thing, because the higher the price, the higher the profit of the holding cost will be, and the shape of the two lines will be almost exactly the same. So the higher the price, the higher the RUP will be; the lower the price, the lower the RUP will be. This is very simple. But once the RUP shows a so-called divergence, it actually means that the market situation has changed. What is divergence? For example, Bitcoin rose to 90,000, and then fell back to 100,000, creating a higher high point, but when the RUP was 100,000, it was not as high as when it was 90,000, but went down. This is the so-called RUP lowered, but the price became higher. It is very strange why this situation occurred? The only logic that can reasonably explain this matter is that we just talked about that RUP is calculated based on unrealized profits. The main unrealized profits in the market are actually contributed by those low-cost chips. For example, if you bought a bitcoin at 16,000 today, and it is now 96,000, the floating profit of this bitcoin alone is 80,000. But if you bought a bitcoin at 86,000 today, and it is now 96,000, this one is only 10,000, so the main contribution in terms of proportion is made by those low-cost chips. So once your price is higher, but the RUP is lower, it means that a part or even a large amount of low-cost chips must have been sold in advance, resulting in your subsequent higher price. Because these low-cost chips have left the market, they have turned part of the unrealized profits into realized profits, so they cannot be seen on the RUP, which will lead to a lower RUP and a deviation. This can help me get a verification in interpreting the RUP, that is, there are indeed low-cost chips leaving the market.

For the third aspect, there is actually a lot to talk about on-chain data, but I personally share another unique point of view, called the US stock market. If someone has studied the stock market, they will know that there is a so-called valuation concept in the stock market, that is, the price-earnings ratio, or the price-earnings ratio. There are many different variations of this valuation method. The indicator I personally refer to is called Shiller ECY. This indicator comes from Professor Shiller of Yale University. He measures the yield of the stock target relative to the bond target. This indicator was mentioned in a paper he published after the outbreak of the epidemic in 2020. Because he believes that his previous model or data is called Shiller PE, called Shiller Price-Earnings Ratio. He believes that after the epidemic, because of the changes in the structure of the global market, many conditions of that model are actually different from before, so he invented a new indicator called Shiller ECY to measure this market, and then found that this indicator has a better prediction effect. In a nutshell, this indicator currently shows that the valuation of the US stock market is a bit too high. Here I want to clarify one thing. A high valuation does not mean that it must fall. After the valuation is high, it can be higher, and even higher. But it measures a concept similar to a spectrum, that is, it is getting closer and closer to the danger zone. In fact, the position it is approaching now is a relatively dangerous position in my opinion. The valuation of the stock market is currently mainly contributed by the hottest topic, that is, AI. Some time ago, there was a DeepSeek, which suddenly appeared unexpectedly and caused a sudden downward revision of the valuation of the US stock market. But in fact, on this point, I am pessimistic in the short and medium term. Because although DeepSeek is a chip decline in the long run, it is definitely good for the AI ​​industry, but in the short term, I don’t think this valuation effect will end so quickly, so I think there is still room for downward revision of valuation. If the US stock market is not good, then Bitcoin, as a younger brother, will naturally not look good. But these are my personal biases, personal biases, for your reference.

Alex:Okay, Colin just talked about it in great detail, let’s briefly sort out his views. He believes that the current price range has met many of the conditions for the valuation peak or price peak in the past, including the distribution of chips and the unrealized profit ratio he just mentioned, as well as the ECY indicator of Professor Shiller in the traditional financial market. He believes that it currently meets many signs of peaking.

How to get started with on-chain data analysis

Alex:Today we have talked a lot about the analysis principles of on-chain data, including how to observe some common data and how to use these data in practice. Many of our listeners may not have studied this concept or system in depth before. So suppose a beginner asks you for advice, saying Colin, I think what you said today is very attractive to me, and I also want to learn this knowledge from scratch, and guide myself to make some BTC investments. What kind of learning advice would you give them to help them start this period of learning?

Colin：Okay, actually I have received dozens of private messages asking similar questions so far. My personal advice has always been the same. First of all, I have two main strengths. The first strength is on-chain data, and the second strength I think is in the field of technical analysis. In fact, when most people come to ask me, they usually hold a line chart and draw some morphology or an indicator, such as MACD or RSI. They use these things to ask me if there is a way to match this thing with the on-chain data point of view. In fact, I must give a suggestion here first. I personally do not recommend that novices start learning from the field of technical analysis. The main reason is very simple, because there are too many schools of thought, and some of the views in many schools cannot withstand the test of science. Because they are simply inductive methods, there is no logic behind them, and it is easy to go back to the example of the dog barking and heavy rain that I just talked about. In fact, it is entirely possible that it is a survivor bias, but the average novice is unable to distinguish whether this is really useful or just a survivor bias. My personal suggestion is that on-chain data is a very suitable field for novices. I will mention the learning method later. I think the reason why it is suitable for novices is very simple.
